articles.title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
articles.description:
The crowd was part of the nationwide demonstration and heard speeches from state leaders and a performance by the Dropkick Murphys.

The post Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Tens of thousands rallied at Boston Common for the third 'No Kings' protest.
State leaders delivered speeches, and the Dropkick Murphys performed for attendees.
The demonstration highlights growing local opposition to perceived authoritarianism in governance.

articles.title: GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues
articles.description:
The heavy metal band played a 90 minute set including a cover of Chappell Roan's "Pink Pony Club."

The post GWAR gives fanbase a bloody good time at House of Blues appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: GWAR performed a 90-minute set at House of Blues on March 28, 2026.
The band included a cover of Chappell Roan's 'Pink Pony Club' during the show.
Local fans enjoyed a unique heavy metal experience, enhancing Boston's vibrant music scene.

articles.title: Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup
articles.description:
Four players, including Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum, are listed as questionable.

The post Celtics post lengthy injury report ahead of Sunday’s Hornets matchup appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Celtics list four players as questionable for Sunday’s game against the Hornets.
Jaylen Brown and Jayson Tatum are among the key players affected.
Injuries may impact Celtics' performance and playoff positioning in the upcoming games.

articles.title: ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up
articles.description:
From veterans and church groups to costumed protesters and dog owners, demonstrators on Boston Common said they felt compelled to speak out.

The post ‘No Kings’ protest: What protesters said and why they showed up appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Protesters gathered on Boston Common to voice opposition against perceived monarchy influences on March 28, 2026.
Participants included veterans, church groups, and costumed protesters, highlighting diverse community concerns.
Local residents expressed solidarity, emphasizing the need for democratic representation in governance.
